cot_prefix: Okay, let's take a look at the question and the passage provided. The question is asking what upset Melanie during the National Championships. First, I need to find the part of the passage that describes Melanie's experience at the National Championships. The passage says: "Melanie entered her first triathlon at 14 and she won the junior section. Full of confidence, she entered the National Championships, and although she had the second fastest swim and the fastest run, she came nowhere. 'I was following this man and suddenly we came to the sea. We realised then that we had gone wrong. I ended up cycling 20 kilometres too far. I cried all the way through the running.'" So Melanie entered the National Championships and expected to do well because she had the second fastest swim and fastest run. However, she ended up not placing well (she came nowhere). What happened? According to her quote, she was following another competitor (a man) and they made a wrong turn, which led to her cycling 20 kilometers too far. This mistake must have cost her the race and caused her to cry during the running section. The answer choices are: (A) She was tricked by another competitor. (B) She felt she had let her teammates down. (C) She made a mistake during part of the race. (D) She realized she couldn't cycle as fast as she thought. Let's analyze each option: - (A) She was tricked by another competitor: The passage says she was following a man, but there's no indication that he intentionally tricked her. It seems like she both made a mistake and the other person might have done the same, but not that he deliberately tricked her. - (B) She felt she had let her teammates down: The passage doesn't mention her feeling this way. - (C) She made a mistake during part of the race: Yes, she realized they had gone wrong and ended up
